name: My lungs are messed up from chemicals at work in Salt Lake City, do I qualify for workers comp?
description: Yes, chemical-induced lung issues from work qualify as occupational disease if medically linked to your job.[9]

mainEntity.name: My lungs are messed up from chemicals at work in Salt Lake City, do I qualify for workers comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, chemical-induced lung issues from work qualify as occupational disease if medically linked to your job.[9]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, if your lung condition is caused by workplace chemical exposure, it qualifies as a compensable occupational disease under Utah workers' compensation law.[1][9] Utah law covers illnesses from work conditions, including repetitive exposure or toxic substances, without needing a single 'accident'.[9] Key factors: (1) medical evidence linking illness to job duties; (2) symptoms arose from work environment; (3) reported timely to employer. Document doctor visits and exposure details immediately, then file a claim with the Utah Labor Commission. Consult a specialist to strengthen causation proof. Get evaluated by a workers' comp attorney promptly.
